answer:
Lark uses an access token mechanism to authenticate API calls [1][2]. To authenticate, you must place your access token into the HTTP request header using the Bearer authentication scheme [3][1].

The header should be formatted as follows:

`Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`

There are three primary types of access tokens available:
- tenant_access_token (prefixed with t-): Used for performing actions as an app [3][1].
- user_access_token (prefixed with u-): Used for performing actions on behalf of a specific user [3][1].
- app_access_token (prefixed with a- or t-): Used for identifying the app itself [3][1].

Below is an example of an API request using a tenant_access_token:

```bash
curl --location --request GET 'https://open.larksuite.com/open-apis/contact/v3/departments/od-64242a18099d3a31acd24d8fce8d9f9c' \
--header 'Authorization: REDACTED_EXAMPLE' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8'
```

Each specific API endpoint documentation will specify which token type is required and how to obtain it [3][1].
